focus: [D3-A3/D3] registry-as-source-of-truth is fictional at runtime; resolve never-hardcode vs hardcoded-default by making registry router policy the source and generated projections the runtime surfaces

graphEvents.label: What does registry-as-source-of-truth mean when advisor activation and hub mode selection do not read the registry at runtime?
mode-registry is identity and projection data, not a live runtime mode router
The interface default is hardcoded in hub prose while the registry has no routerPolicy default
Advisor can watch or score registry path metadata but does not parse registry aliases or mode policy for routing
Existing packet router replay gates parseability but parent hubs need a registry-router adapter
Registry source-of-truth is enforceable only when registry fields generate or drift-check every runtime projection that claims to route design modes

label: What does registry-as-source-of-truth mean when advisor activation and hub mode selection do not read the registry at runtime?
mode-registry is identity and projection data, not a live runtime mode router
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for static/projection tests; ADVISORY for open-ended live prompt interpretation
recommendation: Define registry identity, generated projections, and runtime surfaces as separate layers; add projection tests from registry to graph metadata, commands, hub fixtures, and packet paths.

label: The interface default is hardcoded in hub prose while the registry has no routerPolicy default
recommendation: Add routerPolicy.defaultMode, hintOverride, ambiguityPolicy, maxBundleModes, and routerSignals to the registry or a generated sibling router file.

label: Advisor can watch or score registry path metadata but does not parse registry aliases or mode policy for routing
recommendation: Keep advisor activation single-identity; validate that registry wake aliases are projected into graph metadata or SKILL keywords, then run hub-router replay for mode selection.

label: Existing packet router replay gates parseability but parent hubs need a registry-router adapter
recommendation: Add hub-registry-router benchmark mode that returns activatedSkill, workflowMode, packet, routeTrace and deferReason, then runs existing packet router replay only against the selected packet.

label: Registry source-of-truth is enforceable only when registry fields generate or drift-check every runtime projection that claims to route design modes

direction: Make skill-advisor directly choose sk-design sub-modes from mode-registry.json
reason: The advisor contract and registry both route natural-language design work to the single sk-design identity; mode selection belongs to the hub projection, not advisor scoring.
